summary.intent: Implement opt-in model availability checking for the moltis project (issue #577): remove automatic background probing after provider setup, add a cancellation token mechanism, and add a frontend Stop button.
summary.outcome: All changes were implemented and verified: 4 auto-probe tokio::spawn blocks removed, CancellationToken-based cancel_detect added to the Rust backend, Stop button added to the frontend, i18n keys added for en/fr/zh, and all quality gates (cargo fmt, clippy, tests, biome) passed. Changes were committed, pushed, and a PR was created.

repo: The project uses a nightly Rust toolchain (nightly-2025-11-30) and a justfile for lint/test tasks.
llama-cpp-sys requires CUDA/CMake which is unavailable on macOS — full `just lint` fails on Mac; per-crate clippy must be used instead.
tokio-util was already declared in the workspace Cargo.toml, so only the crate-level Cargo.toml needed updating.
JS files use tabs for indentation and biome for formatting/linting.
WRITE_METHODS in crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs is where new mutating RPC methods must be registered alongside their handler registration in services.rs.
The fr and zh locale files contained English strings (not yet translated) — new keys were added in English to match existing style.

code.finding: model_probe_params() helper was only used by the 4 auto-probe spawn blocks; safe to delete entirely once those blocks are removed.
WRITE_METHODS array is where cancel_detect (and models.test) live; new mutating RPC methods must be added here.

detect_supported uses a JoinSet/FuturesUnordered probe loop; cancellation was added via tokio::select! watching a stored CancellationToken in an Arc<RwLock<Option<CancellationToken>>> field on LiveModelService.
MockModel delegates all RPC methods via self.0.call(); cancel_detect follows the same pattern as other delegated methods.

workflow: When adding a new dependency to a workspace crate, run `cargo update` or `cargo check` to sync Cargo.lock before running lint checks that include a lockfile-check step.
Removing an import (Arc) prematurely without verifying all usages caused a compilation error; always grep for usages before removing imports.
Running per-crate `cargo clippy -p <crate>` is the reliable workaround when full-workspace lint fails due to unrelated native build dependencies.

friction: Prematurely removed the `Arc` import from services.rs — it was still used elsewhere in the file, causing a compilation error that required a follow-up edit.
`just lint` fails on macOS due to llama-cpp-sys requiring CUDA/CMake, forcing per-crate clippy as a workaround.
Tab-based indentation in JS files made Edit tool matches fragile — required reading raw file content and a bash hex-dump step to get exact whitespace for the progress bar edit.
No existing PR for the branch meant the 'Fix PR comment' request was a no-op until the user clarified they actually wanted commit + push + PR creation.
open_items: Manual QA still needed: connect a provider via OAuth/API key and verify no automatic probing starts, verify 'Detect All Models' still works, and verify the Stop button cancels mid-detection.
French and Chinese locale strings were added in English — proper translations for stopDetection and detectionCancelled are deferred.
run_single_probe internals were intentionally left unchanged; spawned tasks detach on cancel and rely on the existing 20s timeout for cleanup rather than explicit cancellation propagation.
